title: Cipher context is not implicitly reset for updates
problem: The JCE `Cipher` specification requires the cipher to be implicitly reset after `doFinal`. Since `doFinalStreaming` frees the context and sets `ctx = 0`, a subsequent `update` call will incorrectly throw an `IllegalStateException` instead of re-initializing the context.

title: Cipher context is not implicitly reset for doFinal
problem: The JCE `Cipher` specification requires the cipher to be implicitly reset after `doFinal`. A subsequent `doFinal` call will incorrectly throw an `IllegalStateException` because `ctx` is 0. Re-initializing the context lazily allows subsequent operations without explicit re-initialization.

title: Key handle leak in SM4 streaming operations
problem: The imported symmetric key handle is not stored in `SM4Context`, meaning it is never destroyed when the streaming encryption/decryption context is freed. This causes a memory and resource leak on the SDF cryptographic hardware device.

title: Missing key handle storage in SM4 encrypt init
problem: The successfully imported key handle must be stored in the context so it can be destroyed later.

title: Missing key handle storage in SM4 decrypt init
problem: The successfully imported key handle must be stored in the context so it can be destroyed later.

title: Missing SDF_DestroyKey in SM4 free
problem: The imported key handle must be destroyed to free hardware device resources when the cipher context is closed.

title: Race condition in native library extraction
problem: Using a static temporary file name can cause concurrent write conflicts and corrupted native libraries if multiple JVMs initialize the provider simultaneously.

title: engineGetOutputSize ignores buffered data size
problem: According to the CipherSpi contract, `engineGetOutputSize` must account for any data currently held in the internal buffer. Failing to include `buffer.size()` can cause the caller to allocate too small an output array.

title: engineGetOutputSize ignores buffered data size
problem: According to the CipherSpi contract, `engineGetOutputSize` must account for any data currently held in the internal buffer. Failing to include `buffer.size()` can cause the caller to allocate too small an output array.

title: Incorrect exception type for empty plaintext in SM2 encrypt
problem: When `plainLen` is 0, the code throws `OutOfMemoryError` instead of `IllegalArgumentException`, which is misleading for developers trying to debug.
